full_text:
Resubmission planning - comments and thoughts for going forward

Due to the rejection and Science Advances, we need to prepare the manuscript for resubmission in another journal. As the reviewer comments largely focus on the sample numbers and the robustness of the findings, we have to critically assess the entire manuscript to be able to turn it into a publishable story.

Before we meet during week 46, we all (Anniina, Joonas, Maria, Matilda) should go through the manuscript critically, and assess its strengths and weaknesses in order to make it better.

Some questions to lead into the discussion on what we should change in the manuscript in order to prepare it for resubmission:

What are the advantages and strengths of our model vs. other similar models?

Why do we not want to go deeper into FACS etc. validation for our model?

Which journal should we aim for next?

New title: Platform for multimodal functional testing and biomarker discovery?

Joonas

1. Our model is very clean in that we do not introduce additional cell components to the cultures in successive steps, we utilize only the material directly obtained from the patient. Also, cultrex has a quite small batch variation in its composition. However, is cultrex the most relevant for our purposes?

2. The current validations are problematic due to the high disparity between validation methods. I dont think introducing new data types will improve anything. I would more focus on why the current discrepancies exist and can we do something about them using our existing data

3. Clinical Cancer Res?

JCI Insight?

EMBO Mol Med?

4. Depends on what we display of the drug screening results (all, or do we cut back on some aspect?), and do we trust those results?

Matilda

- Omentum gel matrix provides a more disease-specific growth environment for the cancer cells vs BME

- The preservation of immune cells from the patient samples (BUT how much do we trust the results from the immune cell scorings?)

We already have a bunch of validation methods in use, can we strengthen the proof from these instead of performing more validations? I think introducing more validations will only make the story more confusing.

Cell Reports 2020:
Patient-Derived Ovarian Cancer Organoids Mimic Clinical Response and Exhibit Heterogeneous Inter- and Intrapatient Drug Responses

https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32553164/

WGS data, no imaging results

I like the new title suggestion, I don’t think the ATRi results should be the focus of the paper. For me, the results I feel least confident in are the imaging data results in Fig 4

The amount of data we have is massive, and it was very difficult to normalise the data

Reviewer 3: “The absence of biaxial plots or histograms is problematic, especially since the observed fold changes are very small (approximately log2FC = 0.2). A slight modification in gating could potentially result in fold changes of a similar magnitude.”

None of the key people involved in this analysis work in the lab anymore (Ashwini, Aditi)

Maria
